hero.eyebrow: Sustainable tourism for Ham Ninh fishing village
hero.title: Ham Ninh AI Guide — Preserve Heritage. Support Tourism. Fairness for Fishermen.
hero.description: A multi-agent AI concept combining cultural-preservation RAG and ensemble re-ranking to support local livelihoods in Ham Ninh fishing village, Phu Quoc.

trustBadges.label: Fair re-ranking
trustBadges.description: Adds local business signals to recommendation order.

trustBadges.label: Bilingual content
trustBadges.description: Vietnamese and English content share the same structure.

problem.heading: Tourism can grow quickly while local value is left behind
problem.body: When large platforms optimize for popularity, Ham Ninh’s cultural stories and small local businesses need a fairer introduction layer.

cards.title: Over-tourism & cultural erosion
cards.description: Fragmented information can reduce the village to photo spots without explaining its fishing heritage.

cards.title: Platform economic bias
cards.description: Businesses with more reviews or marketing budgets often outrank local family-run providers.

cards.title: Missing accurate local sources
cards.description: Cultural answers should come from retrieved documents and citations, not model guesswork.

solution.heading: A multi-agent system connecting culture, maps, and algorithmic fairness
solution.body: The landing page introduces the target architecture: RAG for local knowledge, a Maps Agent for place data, and an Ensemble Re-ranker for responsible ranking.

pillars.title: RAG Agent — Local Guide
pillars.description: Answers culture, history, and food questions using retrieved sources.

pillars.title: Maps & Service Agent — Concierge
pillars.description: Planned to connect with Places API for places, routes, and accessibility context.

pillars.title: Ensemble Re-ranker — Fairness Engine
pillars.description: Combines Random Forest and Gradient Boosting to balance quality, distance, and local_factor.

responsibleAI.heading: Five control axes from data to user experience
responsibleAI.body: Each axis maps to a metric or verification mechanism that reduces risk as chat and map functionality arrive in later slices.

axes.title: Reliability
axes.description: Cultural answers should stay grounded in retrieved sources and cite them.

axes.title: Bias & fairness
axes.description: Place ranking considers local_factor to support local businesses.

axes.title: Robustness
axes.description: Guardrails, topic filters, and fallbacks reduce dependence on a single failure point.

axes.title: Social impact
axes.description: Prioritizes small vendors, elders, and accessibility needs.

axes.title: Explainability
axes.description: Reasoning logs, citations, and score breakdowns help users understand recommendations.

algorithmShowcase.heading: Ensemble re-ranking turns place signals into a fairer order
algorithmShowcase.body: Shows how a Decision Tree grows into Random Forest bagging, Gradient Boosting, and a final explainable score.

chart.title: Illustrative contribution to Final Score
chart.ariaLabel: Bar chart showing example contributions of local factor, rating, and distance to the final score.
chart.description: The values below are interface explanation data, not live Places API results.

bars.label: Local factor
bars.description: How strongly a place is tied to local family businesses and traditional livelihoods.

bars.label: Rating
bars.description: Perceived quality from normalized rating and review signals.

bars.label: Distance
bars.description: Travel convenience from the user location or planned route.

steps.title: Decision Tree
steps.description: Learns simple thresholds over rating, distance, and local_factor.

steps.title: Random Forest
steps.description: Bags many trees to reduce variance when place data is noisy.

steps.title: Gradient Boosting
steps.description: Sequentially learns remaining errors to reduce ranking bias.

steps.title: Ensemble Score
steps.description: Combines 0.6 × RF and 0.4 × GBM into an explainable final_score.

techStack.heading: Academic stack, designed for real operation
techStack.body: The components support i18n, knowledge retrieval, agent orchestration, RAG evaluation, and system observability.

items.name: Next.js 16
items.description: Frontend App Router, locale routes, and responsive landing UI.

items.name: FastAPI
items.description: API gateway for chat, SSE, and health checks.

items.name: LangGraph
items.description: Supervisor and worker agent orchestration with checkpoints.

items.name: Qdrant
items.description: Vector database for Ham Ninh cultural RAG.

items.name: Google Maps Platform
items.description: Place and routing data source in later slices.

items.name: scikit-learn
items.description: Random Forest and Gradient Boosting for re-ranking.

items.name: RAGAS
items.description: Evaluates faithfulness, relevance, and context recall.

items.name: Langfuse
items.description: Traces, latency, and cost for agent workflows.

demo.heading: The experience shell opens progressively by slice
demo.body: In this slice, the landing page describes target capabilities. Real chat, map, and architecture functionality will be connected in later slices.
demo.note: CTAs may lead to scaffold or in-progress pages; they do not imply fully working chat/map behavior before S03.

items.title: Citation-backed chat
items.description: Planned to show RAG answers with sources and a reasoning log.

items.title: Re-ranked map
items.description: Planned to show Places API results after fairness scoring.

items.title: Transparent architecture
items.description: Planned to explain the supervisor, agents, guardrails, and observability.
